Let me draft:

Paragraph 1 - opens with "First":
First, Will wakes early on Saturday morning. He makes coffee in the kitchen and packs lunch for the child's field trip. If the car runs well, then the child will have a good day. Will checks the oil and tightens a loose bolt under the hood.

Paragraph 2:
The child comes to the garage and watches Will work. Will wipes his hands on a towel and smiles. The child asks, "Do you ever get tired of all this work?" Will says that duty is not a burden when it helps the people he loves.

Paragraph 3 - opens with "After" or "After that":
After that, Will and the child plant a small tree near the fence. The old tree fell last winter. The child holds the tree steady while Will packs dirt around its roots. The child tells Will that helping feels good.

Paragraph 4 - opens with "Finally":
Finally, Will nods and says that caring for things is what adulthood really means. A grown-up does these tasks because they matter to the family. If the tree grows tall, then it will shade the yard for years.

[Ninereeds]First, Will wakes early on Saturday morning. He makes coffee in the kitchen and packs lunch for the child's field trip. If the car runs well, then the child will have a good day. Will checks the oil and tightens a loose bolt under the hood.

After that, Will and the child plant a small tree near the fence. The old tree fell last winter. The child holds the tree steady while Will packs dirt around its roots. The child tells Will that helping feels good.

Finally, Will nods and says that caring for things is what adulthood really means. A grown-up does these tasks because they matter to the family. If the tree grows tall, then it will shade the yard for years.
